records.name: AFIELDS
records.summary: Deprecated compatibility function that fills one or more supplied arrays with field-structure information from the current work area.
records.search_text: AFIELDS AFIELDS Deprecated Deprecated Field Functions AFields([<@aFieldNames>],[<@aTypes>],[<@aWidths>],[<@aDecimals>],[<@aFlags>]) => <nFields> Deprecated compatibility function that fills one or more supplied arrays with field-structure information from the current work area. Deprecated compatibility function that fills one or more supplied arrays with field-structure information from the current work area. `<aFieldNames>` receives field names. `<aTypes>` receives field type codes. `<aWidths>` receives field widths. `<aDecimals>` receives decimal counts. `<aFlags>` receives field flags when the Harbour build exposes that metadata. The function returns the number of fields copied into the supplied arrays. If no current work area exists, or if no output array is supplied, it returns `0`. Prefer `dbStruct()` for new code because it returns the field structure as a single multidimensional array. hbrdd dbstruct aFieldNames Array Array that receives field names from the current work area. aTypes Array Array that receives field type codes from the current work area. aWidths Array Array that receives field widths from the current work area. aDecimals Array Array that receives field decimal counts from the current work area. aFlags Array Optional Harbour-extension array that receives field flags when supported by the build and RDD. Numeric nFields Number of field entries copied into the supplied arrays. Returns `0` if no current work area exists or if no output array is supplied.

records.name: DBFIELDINF
records.summary: Returns or optionally changes information about a field in the current work area through the active RDD.
records.search_text: DBFIELDINF DBFIELDINF Field Functions Field Functions Table Functions dbFieldInfo=>dbFieldInfo(<nInfoType>,<nFieldPos>,[<xNewSetting>]) => <xFieldInfo> Returns or optionally changes information about a field in the current work area through the active RDD. Returns or optionally changes information about a field in the current work area through the active RDD. `<nInfoType>` selects which field information value to retrieve or set. Available values are defined in `dbinfo.ch`; some field-structure constants are also related to `dbstruct.ch`. `<nFieldPos>` is a one-based field position. `<xNewSetting>` is optional and is passed to the RDD as the proposed new value for info types that support changing a field setting. A current work area must be open. If no work area is selected, Harbour raises a database-command runtime error. If `<nInfoType>` is not numeric or `<nFieldPos>` is outside the current field range, Harbour raises a database-command argument error. dbinfo.ch hbrdd dbinfo Any xFieldInfo The value returned by the active RDD for the requested field information. The type and meaning depend on `<nInfoType>` and RDD support. If no current work area is open, `<nInfoType>` is invalid, or `<nFieldPos>` is outside the current field range, a runtime database-command error is raised.

A current work area must be open. If no work area is selected, Harbour raises a database-command runtime error. If `<nInfoType>` is not numeric, Harbour raises a database-command argument error. dbinfo.ch hbrdd dbinfo recno nInfoType Numeric Numeric record information selector. Use constants from `dbinfo.ch`. xRecord Any Optional record selector passed to the active RDD. xNewSetting Any Optional proposed new value for info types that support changing a record setting. The value is passed to the active RDD. Any xCurrentSetting The value returned by the active RDD for the requested record information. The type and meaning depend on `<nInfoType>` and RDD support. If no current work area is open or `<nInfoType>` is invalid, a runtime database-command error is raised.

records.name: hb_CStr
records.summary: Converts a Harbour value to a compact human-readable string.
records.search_text: hb_CStr hb_CStr Conversion Functions Conversion Functions String Codepage Functions hb_CStr(<xVal>) => <cText> Converts a Harbour value to a compact human-readable string. Converts a Harbour value to a compact human-readable string. Strings and memos are returned as-is; numerics use `Str()`; dates and timestamps use literal-style text; logicals use `.T.` or `.F.`; complex values such as arrays, hashes, objects, pointers, symbols, codeblocks, and NIL use descriptive text. hbrtl hb_valtoexp hb_valtostr xVal Any Value to convert to compact readable text. String cText Compact readable string representation of `<xVal>`.

records.name: hb_DirBuild
records.summary: Creates a directory hierarchy, creating missing parent directories as needed.
records.search_text: hb_DirBuild hb_DirBuild Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders hb_DirBuild(<cDir>) => <lSuccess> Creates a directory hierarchy, creating missing parent directories as needed. Creates a directory hierarchy, creating missing parent directories as needed. `<cDir>` is normalized before use. The function returns `.T.` when the requested directory already exists or the missing hierarchy was successfully created. It returns `.F.` when `<cDir>` is not a string, when an intermediate path component exists as a file, or when a directory creation step fails. hbrtl hb_dircreate hb_direxists hb_dirunbuild cDir String Directory hierarchy to create or verify. Logic lSuccess `.T.` if the directory hierarchy exists or was created successfully; otherwise `.F.`.

Numeric nResult `0` on success; otherwise a filesystem error code or `F_ERROR`.

records.name: HB_DIREXISTS

records.search_text: HB_DIREXISTS HB_DIREXISTS Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders hb_DirExists(<cDir>) => <lExists> Tests whether a directory exists with the supplied directory name. Tests whether a directory exists with the supplied directory name. hbrtl hb_dirbuild hb_fileexists hb_fnameexists cDir String Directory name to test. records.name: hb_DirRemoveAll
records.summary: Recursively removes a directory and its contents.
records.search_text: hb_DirRemoveAll hb_DirRemoveAll Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders hb_DirRemoveAll(<cDir>) => <lSuccess> Recursively removes a directory and its contents. Recursively removes a directory and its contents. If `<cDir>` is empty or does not exist as a directory, the function returns `.T.`. When the directory exists, read-only attributes are cleared where possible before removal. Subdirectories are removed recursively, except symbolic-link directories are treated as entries rather than traversed. The function returns `.F.` when an erase or directory-remove operation fails. fileio.ch hbrtl hb_dirbuild hb_vferase cDir String Directory path to remove recursively. Logic lSuccess `.T.` if the directory was removed or did not exist; `.F.` if a file or directory removal step fails.

records.name: hb_DirScan
records.summary: Recursively scans a directory tree and returns matching directory entries.
records.search_text: hb_DirScan hb_DirScan Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders hb_DirScan([<cPath>],[<cFileMask>],[<cAttr>]) => <aDirectory> Recursively scans a directory tree and returns matching directory entries. Recursively scans a directory tree and returns matching directory entries. `<cPath>` defaults to a null string, `<cFileMask>` defaults to the platform all-files mask when not supplied as a string, and `<cAttr>` defaults to a null string. The returned array uses the same entry structure as `hb_vfDirectory()` / `Directory()` style directory arrays. File names returned from subdirectories are prefixed with the relative subdirectory path. directry.ch hbrtl directory hb_directory hb_vfdirectory cPath String Starting directory path. Defaults to the current relative root when omitted or invalid. cFileMask String File mask used to match file names. Defaults to the platform all-files mask when omitted or invalid. cAttr String Optional directory attribute mask. Include `D` to return matching directories as entries. Array aDirectory Array of directory entry arrays. Entries from subdirectories have relative subdirectory prefixes in their file-name element.

records.name: hb_DirSepAdd
records.summary: Returns a directory path with a trailing platform path separator when one should be added.
records.search_text: hb_DirSepAdd hb_DirSepAdd Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders File Functions hb_DirSepAdd(<cDir>) => <cDir> Returns a directory path with a trailing platform path separator when one should be added. Returns a directory path with a trailing platform path separator when one should be added. If `<cDir>` is not a string, the function returns a null string. A separator is added only when the string is non-empty, is not a drive-only specification, and does not already end with the platform path separator. hbrtl hb_dirsepdel hb_dirseptoos hb_ps cDir String Directory path to adjust. String cDir Directory path with a trailing path separator when applicable; otherwise the original string or a null string for invalid input.

Returns a directory path with unnecessary trailing platform path separators removed. If `<cDir>` is not a string, the function returns a null string. The function preserves root paths, drive roots, and UNC-root-like forms where removing the separator would change the meaning of the path. hbrtl hb_dirsepadd hb_dirseptoos hb_ps cDir String Directory path to adjust. String cDir Directory path with removable trailing path separators removed; otherwise the original string or a null string for invalid input.

If `<cFileName>` is not a string, the function returns a null string. hbrtl hb_dirsepadd hb_dirsepdel hb_ps cFileName String File or path string whose separators should be converted to the current platform separator. records.name: hb_DirUnbuild
records.summary: Removes a directory hierarchy upward from the supplied directory while directories can be deleted.
records.search_text: hb_DirUnbuild hb_DirUnbuild Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders File Functions hb_DirUnbuild(<cDir>) => <lSuccess> Removes a directory hierarchy upward from the supplied directory while directories can be deleted. Removes a directory hierarchy upward from the supplied directory while directories can be deleted. If `<cDir>` is not a string, the function returns `.F.`. If `<cDir>` exists, the function removes that directory and then moves upward through parent directories until deletion fails, the path becomes empty, no path separator remains, or a drive root is reached. If the initial directory does not exist, the function returns `.T.`. hbrtl hb_dirbuild hb_dirdelete hb_direxists cDir String Directory path to remove upward from. Logic lSuccess `.T.` if the directory hierarchy was removed as far as possible or the starting directory did not exist; `.F.` if input is invalid or a directory delete operation fails.

Gets or changes the global file-lock waiting behavior used by Harbour low-level file locking routines. hbrtl lDisable Logic Optional logical value indicating whether wait locks should be disabled. records.name: HB_DISKSPACE
records.summary: Returns disk-space information for a path, drive, or the default filesystem location.
records.search_text: HB_DISKSPACE HB_DISKSPACE Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders hb_DiskSpace([<cPathOrDrive>],[<nType>]) => <nDiskBytes> Returns disk-space information for a path, drive, or the default filesystem location. Returns disk-space information for a path, drive, or the default filesystem location. `<nType>` selects available, free, used, or total space using constants from `fileio.ch`. fileio.ch hbrtl hb_vfdirspace cPathOrDrive Any Path string, or on drive-letter platforms a numeric drive selector. nType Numeric Disk-space selector such as `HB_DISK_AVAIL`, `HB_DISK_FREE`, `HB_DISK_USED`, or `HB_DISK_TOTAL`. Numeric nDiskBytes Requested disk-space value in bytes, or `0` when the information cannot be obtained.

records.name: HB_DISPBOX
records.summary: Draws a box in a screen region through the GT driver.
records.search_text: HB_DISPBOX HB_DISPBOX Classic UI Classic UI hb_DispBox(<nTop>,<nLeft>,<nBottom>,<nRight>,[<cBoxChars>],[<cnColor>]) => NIL Draws a box in a screen region through the GT driver. Draws a box in a screen region through the GT driver. This Harbour-specific form accepts either box-character text and a color string, or a numeric color value. hbrtl dispbox nTop Numeric Top row of the box region. nLeft Numeric Left column of the box region. nBottom Numeric Bottom row of the box region. nRight Numeric Right column of the box region. cBoxChars String Optional box-character string passed to the GT driver. cnColor Any Optional color specification, supplied as a color string or numeric color value. NIL Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_FCOPY

records.search_text: HB_FCOPY HB_FCOPY File Functions File Functions hb_FCopy(<cSourceFile>,<cDestinationFile>) => <nResult> Copies a file from `<cSourceFile>` to `<cDestinationFile>` using Harbour file APIs. Logic lEof `.T.` when the file is at end-of-file or when the argument is invalid; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_FGETDATETIME

records.search_text: HB_FGETDATETIME HB_FGETDATETIME Date and Time Functions Date and Time Functions File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_FGetDateTime(<cFileName>,<@tsDateTime>,[<@cTime>]) => <lOk> Retrieves the date/time timestamp for a file. When supplied by reference, Harbour stores the date in parameter 2 and the time string in this parameter. Logic lOk Returns `.T.` if the timestamp is retrieved successfully; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: hb_FileDelete
records.summary: Deletes files matching `<cFileMask>` and returns whether at least one file was deleted.
records.search_text: hb_FileDelete hb_FileDelete File Functions File Functions hb_FileDelete(<cFileMask>,[<cAttr>]) => <lDeleted> Deletes files matching `<cFileMask>` and returns whether at least one file was deleted. Deletes files matching `<cFileMask>` and returns whether at least one file was deleted. `<cFileMask>` may include a path and wildcard mask. Directories are not deleted. Optional `<cAttr>` controls the directory attribute mask used for matching; `D` is removed from that mask and symbolic links are included. Read-only files are made writable before deletion when possible. hbrtl hb_dirremoveall hb_fileexists hb_vferase cFileMask String File name or file mask to delete. May include a path. cAttr String Optional attribute mask used when finding matching files. Logic lDeleted `.T.` if at least one matching file was deleted; otherwise `.F.`.

Logic lExists `.T.` if the file exists; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_FSETDATETIME

records.search_text: HB_FSETDATETIME HB_FSETDATETIME Date and Time Functions Date and Time Functions File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_FSetDateTime(<cFileName>,<xDateTime>,[<cTime>]) => <lOk> Sets the date and time of a file. Sets the date and time of a file. `<xDateTime>` may be a Harbour timestamp or a date. If a date is supplied, `<cTime>` may optionally supply the time string, including milliseconds when supported by the parser. The function updates `FError()`. hbrtl hb_fgetdatetime cFileName String File name whose timestamp is set. xDateTime Any Timestamp value, or date value when `<cTime>` is supplied separately. cTime String Optional time string used when `<xDateTime>` is a date value. Logic lOk Returns `.T.` if the file timestamp is set successfully; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_FSETDEVMODE

records.search_text: HB_FSETDEVMODE HB_FSETDEVMODE Low Level File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_FSetDevMode(<nHandle>,<nDevMode>) => <nResult> Sets the device mode for a numeric file handle and returns the backend result code. Sets the device mode for a numeric file handle and returns the backend result code. If `<nHandle>` is not numeric, the function returns `-1` and sets `FError()` to `6`. hbrtl nHandle Numeric Numeric file handle. nDevMode Numeric Device mode value passed to the filesystem layer. Numeric nResult Backend result code, or `-1` when the handle argument is invalid.

records.name: HB_FSIZE
records.summary: Returns the size of a file in bytes.
records.search_text: HB_FSIZE HB_FSIZE File Functions File Functions hb_FSize(<cFileName>,[<lUseDirEntry>]) => <nBytes> Returns the size of a file in bytes. Returns the size of a file in bytes. By default Harbour uses directory-entry/stat-style information when possible; when `<lUseDirEntry>` is `.F.`, it opens the file and seeks to the end. hbrtl cFileName String File name to inspect. lUseDirEntry Logic When `.T.`, use directory-entry/stat information when possible; when `.F.`, open and seek the file. Numeric nBytes File size in bytes, or `0` when the file size cannot be obtained.

records.name: HB_INETCOMPRESS

records.search_text: HB_INETCOMPRESS HB_INETCOMPRESS Compression Compression Socket Functions hb_inetCompress(<pInetSocket>,[<nLevel>],[<nStrategy>],[<cKey>]) => <NIL> Enables, changes, or disables ZLIB compression for an `hb_inet*()` connection. Enables, changes, or disables ZLIB compression for an `hb_inet*()` connection. Passing `HB_ZLIB_COMPRESSION_DISABLE` disables compression; otherwise the function opens a ZLIB stream with the selected level and strategy and attaches it to the connection. An optional key enables stream encryption support in the ZNET layer. hbzlib.ch hbrtl hb_inetconnect hb_inetcreate hb_inetrecv hb_inetsend pInetSocket Pointer `hb_inet*()` connection handle. nLevel Numeric ZLIB compression level, or `HB_ZLIB_COMPRESSION_DISABLE` to disable compression. nStrategy Numeric ZLIB compression strategy. cKey String Optional encryption key for the ZNET stream. NIL This function does not return a value. It raises a runtime error if the connection or stream setup fails.

records.name: hb_iniNew
records.summary: Creates and returns a new INI hash.
records.search_text: hb_iniNew hb_iniNew INI Files INI Files hb_iniNew([<lAutoMain>]) => <hIni> Creates and returns a new INI hash. Creates and returns a new INI hash. When `<lAutoMain>` is omitted or `.T.`, the hash is initialized with an empty `"MAIN"` section. When `<lAutoMain>` is `.F.`, the returned hash is empty. hbrtl hb_iniread hb_iniwrite lAutoMain Logic When `.T.`, creates an empty `"MAIN"` section in the returned hash. Hash hIni New INI hash, optionally initialized with a `"MAIN"` section.

records.name: hb_iniRead
records.summary: Reads an INI file into a hash by loading file text and passing it to `hb_iniReadStr()`.
records.search_text: hb_iniRead hb_iniRead INI Files INI Files hb_iniRead(<cFileSpec>,[<lKeyCaseSens>],[<cSplitters>],[<lAutoMain>]) => <hIni> Reads an INI file into a hash by loading file text and passing it to `hb_iniReadStr()`. Reads an INI file into a hash by loading file text and passing it to `hb_iniReadStr()`. `<cFileSpec>` may contain a platform path-list-separated list of possible INI files; the first existing readable file is loaded. If the file cannot be read, the parser receives a null string. The returned hash follows the same structure as `hb_iniReadStr()`. hbrtl hb_ininew hb_inireadstr hb_iniwrite cFileSpec String INI filename or path-list-separated list of possible INI filenames. lKeyCaseSens Logic When `.T.`, keys and section names are case-sensitive. When `.F.`, parsed keys and section names are uppercased. cSplitters String Regular-expression splitter used between keys and values. lAutoMain Logic When `.T.`, top-level keys are stored in a `"MAIN"` section. Hash hIni Hash containing parsed INI data. A `"MAIN"` section is included when `<lAutoMain>` is `.T.`.

records.name: hb_iniReadStr
records.summary: Parses INI-formatted text into a hash.
records.search_text: hb_iniReadStr hb_iniReadStr INI Files INI Files hb_iniReadStr(<cData>,[<lKeyCaseSens>],[<cSplitters>],[<lAutoMain>]) => <hIni> Parses INI-formatted text into a hash. Parses INI-formatted text into a hash. `hb_iniReadStr()` supports full-line comments, inline comments, sections in `[Section]` form, key/value lines split by `<cSplitters>`, `include` lines, and line continuation when a trimmed line ends with ` ||`. When `<lKeyCaseSens>` is `.F.`, section names and keys are uppercased. When `<lAutoMain>` is `.T.`, top-level keys are stored in a `"MAIN"` section. hbrtl hb_ininew hb_iniread hb_iniwritestr cData String INI-formatted text to parse. lKeyCaseSens Logic When `.T.`, keys and section names are case-sensitive. When `.F.`, parsed keys and section names are uppercased. cSplitters String Regular-expression splitter used between keys and values. lAutoMain Logic When `.T.`, top-level keys are stored in a `"MAIN"` section. Hash hIni Hash containing parsed INI data.

records.name: hb_iniSetComment
records.summary: Sets the line-comment and inline-comment markers used by Harbour INI parsing functions.
records.search_text: hb_iniSetComment hb_iniSetComment INI Files INI Files hb_iniSetComment([<cLineComment>],[<cHalfLineComment>]) Sets the line-comment and inline-comment markers used by Harbour INI parsing functions. Sets the line-comment and inline-comment markers used by Harbour INI parsing functions. hbrtl hb_iniread hb_inireadstr cLineComment String Marker used to recognize full-line comments. cHalfLineComment String Marker used to recognize comments that begin within a line.

records.name: hb_iniWrite
records.summary: Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle.
records.search_text: hb_iniWrite hb_iniWrite INI Files INI Files hb_iniWrite(<xFileName>,<hIni>,[<cCommentBegin>],[<cCommentEnd>],[<lAutoMain>]) => <lSuccess> Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle. Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle. `hb_iniWrite()` first calls `hb_iniWriteStr()` to build the INI text. `<xFileName>` may be a filename string, in which case the function creates/overwrites the file and closes it, or a numeric file handle, in which case the handle is used but not closed. The function returns `.F.` if INI text cannot be generated, the file argument is invalid, the file cannot be created/opened, or the full text cannot be written. hbrtl hb_iniread hb_iniwritestr xFileName Any Filename string to create/overwrite, or an existing numeric file handle. hIni Hash INI hash to write. cCommentBegin String Optional comment text written before generated INI content. cCommentEnd String Optional comment text written after generated INI content. lAutoMain Logic Controls how the `"MAIN"` section or top-level entries are written. Logic lSuccess `.T.` if the generated INI text is written successfully; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: hb_iniWriteStr
records.summary: Converts an INI hash into INI-formatted text.
records.search_text: hb_iniWriteStr hb_iniWriteStr INI Files INI Files hb_iniWriteStr(<hIni>,[<cCommentBegin>],[<cCommentEnd>],[<lAutoMain>]) => <cData> Converts an INI hash into INI-formatted text. Converts an INI hash into INI-formatted text. `hb_iniWriteStr()` writes optional leading and trailing comment text, writes top-level key/value pairs or the `"MAIN"` section depending on `<lAutoMain>`, and then writes hash-valued sections in `[Section]` form. If `<lAutoMain>` is `.T.` but the hash has no `"MAIN"` section, it behaves as if `<lAutoMain>` were `.F.`. If `<hIni>` is not a hash, or if no output text is generated, it returns `NIL`. hbrtl hb_ininew hb_iniread hb_inireadstr hb_iniwrite hIni Hash INI data hash to convert to text. cCommentBegin String Optional comment text written before generated INI content when non-empty. cCommentEnd String Optional comment text written after generated INI content when non-empty. lAutoMain Logic Controls how the `"MAIN"` section or top-level entries are written. String cData Generated INI-formatted text. If `NIL` is returned, the input was not a hash or no output text was generated.

records.search_text: HB_ISREGEXSTRING HB_ISREGEXSTRING Regular Expressions Regular Expressions HB_ISREGEXSTRING=>hb_IsRegex([<xValue>]) => <lIsRegex> Returns whether the argument is a compiled regular-expression value. Returns whether the argument is a compiled regular-expression value. hbrtl hb_isregex Logic lIsRegex `.T.` when the test succeeds; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_ISSTRING
records.summary: Returns whether the argument is a string-like character value.
records.search_text: HB_ISSTRING HB_ISSTRING String Byte Functions String Byte Functions String Codepage Functions Type Checking Functions hb_IsString([<xValue>]) => <lIsString> Returns whether the argument is a string-like character value. Returns whether the argument is a string-like character value. hbrtl hb_ischar hb_ismemo valtype xValue Any Value to test. Logic lIsString `.T.` when the test succeeds; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_ISSYMBOL
records.summary: Returns whether the argument is a symbol.
records.search_text: HB_ISSYMBOL HB_ISSYMBOL Symbol Functions Symbol Functions Type Checking Functions hb_IsSymbol([<xValue>]) => <lIsSymbol> Returns whether the argument is a symbol. Returns whether the argument is a symbol. hbrtl valtype xValue Any Value to test. Logic lIsSymbol `.T.` when the test succeeds; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_ISTIMESTAMP
records.summary: Returns whether the argument is a Harbour timestamp value.
records.search_text: HB_ISTIMESTAMP HB_ISTIMESTAMP Date and Time Functions Date and Time Functions Type Checking Functions hb_IsTimeStamp([<xValue>]) => <lIsTimeStamp> Returns whether the argument is a Harbour timestamp value. Returns whether the argument is a Harbour timestamp value. hbrtl hb_isdate hb_isdatetime valtype xValue Any Value to test. Logic lIsTimeStamp `.T.` when the test succeeds; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_JSONDECODE
records.summary: Decodes a JSON string and stores the decoded value into a by-reference variable.
records.search_text: HB_JSONDECODE HB_JSONDECODE Conversion Functions Conversion Functions JSON hb_jsonDecode(<cJSON>,<@xValue>,[<cCPID>]) => <nLengthDecoded> Decodes a JSON string and stores the decoded value into a by-reference variable. Decodes a JSON string and stores the decoded value into a by-reference variable. Returns the number of processed characters/symbols. hbrtl hb_jsonencode cJSON String JSON text to decode. xValue Any Receives decoded Harbour value. cCPID String Optional code page identifier. Numeric nLengthDecoded Number of processed input characters/symbols.

records.name: HB_JSONENCODE
records.summary: Encodes a Harbour value to JSON text.
records.search_text: HB_JSONENCODE HB_JSONENCODE Conversion Functions Conversion Functions JSON hb_jsonEncode(<xValue>,<lHumanOrIndent>,[<cDestCP>]) => <cJSONString> Encodes a Harbour value to JSON text. Encodes a Harbour value to JSON text. Pretty-printing is controlled by a logical human-readable flag or numeric indentation setting; codeblocks encode as JSON null and objects encode as arrays per Harbour's JSON encoder behavior. hbrtl hb_jsondecode xValue Any Value to encode. lHumanOrIndent Any `.T.` for human-readable output, numeric indent value, `0` to disable indentation, or `-1` for tab indentation. cDestCP String Optional destination code page. String cJSONString JSON encoded string.

records.name: HB_KEYCHAR
records.summary: Converts a Harbour key code to the corresponding character string when a character representation is available.
records.search_text: HB_KEYCHAR HB_KEYCHAR Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyChar(<nExtKey>) => <cChar> Converts a Harbour key code to the corresponding character string when a character representation is available. Converts a Harbour key code to the corresponding character string when a character representation is available. hbrtl chr hb_keycode hb_keystd nExtKey Numeric Harbour key code to convert. String cChar Character string for the key code, or a null string when no character representation is available.

records.name: HB_KEYCLEAR
records.summary: Clears Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.
records.search_text: HB_KEYCLEAR HB_KEYCLEAR Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyClear() => NIL Clears Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer. Clears Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer. hbrtl __keyboard hb_keyins hb_keyput NIL Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_KEYCODE
records.summary: Converts the first character of a string to Harbour's extended key code representation.
records.search_text: HB_KEYCODE HB_KEYCODE Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyCode(<cChar>) => <nExtKey> Converts the first character of a string to Harbour's extended key code representation. Converts the first character of a string to Harbour's extended key code representation. Non-ASCII Unicode characters are encoded using Harbour's Unicode key representation. hbrtl asc hb_keychar hb_keystd cChar String String whose first character is converted to a key code. Numeric nExtKey Extended key code for the first character, or `0` when no character can be read.

records.name: HB_KEYEXT
records.summary: Converts a standard key code to Harbour's extended key-code representation.
records.search_text: HB_KEYEXT HB_KEYEXT Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyExt(<nKey>) => <nExtKey> Converts a standard key code to Harbour's extended key-code representation. Converts a standard key code to Harbour's extended key-code representation. hbrtl hb_keycode hb_keymod hb_keystd hb_keyval nKey Numeric Standard key code to convert. Numeric nExtKey Extended Harbour key code.

records.name: HB_KEYINS
records.summary: Inserts a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values at the front of Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.
records.search_text: HB_KEYINS HB_KEYINS Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyIns(<xKey>) => NIL Inserts a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values at the front of Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer. Inserts a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values at the front of Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer. hbrtl hb_keyclear hb_keynext hb_keyput xKey Any Numeric key code, string text, or an array containing numeric/string key values. NIL Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_KEYLAST
records.summary: Returns the last key/event code recorded by Harbour's input layer, optionally constrained by an event mask.
records.search_text: HB_KEYLAST HB_KEYLAST Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyLast([<nEventMask>]) => <nKey> Returns the last key/event code recorded by Harbour's input layer, optionally constrained by an event mask. Returns the last key/event code recorded by Harbour's input layer, optionally constrained by an event mask. hbrtl hb_keynext hb_keysetlast lastkey nEventMask Numeric Optional event mask. Numeric nKey Last key/event code matching the requested mask.

records.name: HB_KEYMOD
records.summary: Returns the modifier-bit portion of a Harbour extended key code.
records.search_text: HB_KEYMOD HB_KEYMOD Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyMod(<nExtKey>) => <nModifiers> Returns the modifier-bit portion of a Harbour extended key code. Returns the modifier-bit portion of a Harbour extended key code. hbrtl hb_keycode hb_keyext hb_keystd hb_keyval nExtKey Numeric Extended Harbour key code. Numeric nModifiers Modifier flags encoded in `<nExtKey>`.

records.name: HB_KEYNEXT
records.summary: Returns the next key/event code from the keyboard buffer without removing it.
records.search_text: HB_KEYNEXT HB_KEYNEXT Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyNext([<nEventMask>]) => <nKey> Returns the next key/event code from the keyboard buffer without removing it. Returns the next key/event code from the keyboard buffer without removing it. hbrtl inkey lastkey nextkey nEventMask Numeric Optional event mask. When omitted, Harbour uses the current SET event mask. Numeric nKey Next key/event code, or `0` if none is available for the requested event mask.

records.name: HB_KEYPUT
records.summary: Appends a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values to Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.
records.search_text: HB_KEYPUT HB_KEYPUT Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyPut(<xKey>) => NIL Appends a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values to Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer. Appends a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values to Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer. hbrtl hb_keyclear hb_keyins hb_keynext xKey Any Numeric key code, string text, or an array containing numeric/string key values. NIL Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_KEYSETLAST
records.summary: Sets Harbour's last-key value and returns the previous last-key value.
records.search_text: HB_KEYSETLAST HB_KEYSETLAST Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keySetLast(<nKeyCode>) => <nPreviousKey> Sets Harbour's last-key value and returns the previous last-key value. Sets Harbour's last-key value and returns the previous last-key value. hbrtl hb_keylast lastkey nKeyCode Numeric New last-key code. Numeric nPreviousKey Previous last-key value. If `<nKeyCode>` is not numeric, no explicit value is returned.

records.name: HB_KEYSTD
records.summary: Converts an extended Harbour key code to the standard Clipper-style key code.
records.search_text: HB_KEYSTD HB_KEYSTD Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyStd(<nExtKey>) => <nClipKey> Converts an extended Harbour key code to the standard Clipper-style key code. Converts an extended Harbour key code to the standard Clipper-style key code. hbrtl hb_keycode hb_keyext hb_keymod hb_keyval nExtKey Numeric Extended Harbour key code. Numeric nClipKey Standard key code corresponding to `<nExtKey>`.

records.name: HB_KEYVAL
records.summary: Returns the key-value portion of a Harbour extended key code.
records.search_text: HB_KEYVAL HB_KEYVAL Keyboard Functions Keyboard Functions hb_keyVal(<nExtKey>) => <nKeyValue> Returns the key-value portion of a Harbour extended key code. Returns the key-value portion of a Harbour extended key code. hbrtl hb_keycode hb_keyext hb_keymod hb_keystd nExtKey Numeric Extended Harbour key code. Numeric nKeyValue Key value encoded in `<nExtKey>`.

records.name: HB_MD5
records.summary: Calculates the MD5 digest of a string.

Restores memory variables from a memo file, optionally adding to existing variables and applying a name mask. hbrtl hb_mvsave cFileName String File name containing saved memory variables. lAdditive Logic When `.T.`, restores variables without first clearing existing public/private variables according to the restore behavior. cMask String Optional memory-variable name mask. lIncludeMask Logic When `.T.`, restores variables matching `<cMask>`; when `.F.`, excludes variables matching `<cMask>`. records.name: hb_PathJoin
records.summary: Combines a base path with a relative path when the second argument is relative.
records.search_text: hb_PathJoin hb_PathJoin Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders File Functions hb_PathJoin(<cPathA>,<cPathR>) => <cPath> Combines a base path with a relative path when the second argument is relative. Combines a base path with a relative path when the second argument is relative. If `<cPathR>` is not a string, the function returns a null string. If `<cPathA>` is not a string or is empty, the function returns `<cPathR>`. If `<cPathR>` already has a drive specification or begins with a path delimiter, it is treated as absolute and returned unchanged. Otherwise, the directory part of `<cPathA>` is combined with `<cPathR>`. hbrtl hb_fnamedir hb_fnamemerge hb_pathnormalize hb_pathrelativize cPathA String Base path used when `<cPathR>` is relative. cPathR String Relative or absolute path to combine with `<cPathA>`. String cPath Combined path when `<cPathR>` is relative; otherwise `<cPathR>` or a null string for invalid `<cPathR>`.

records.name: hb_PathNormalize
records.summary: Normalizes a path string by removing current-directory path elements and resolving ordinary parent-directory pairs.
records.search_text: hb_PathNormalize hb_PathNormalize Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders File Functions hb_PathNormalize(<cPath>) => <cNormalizedPath> Normalizes a path string by removing current-directory path elements and resolving ordinary parent-directory pairs. Normalizes a path string by removing current-directory path elements and resolving ordinary parent-directory pairs. `hb_PathNormalize()` splits `<cPath>` on the platform path separator, removes `.` components, removes redundant empty components where applicable, and collapses a normal directory followed by `..`. If the resulting path is empty after normalization, it returns `.` followed by the platform path separator. If `<cPath>` is not a string, it returns a null string. Source comments note that this normalization can be unsafe when symbolic links are present, because textual `..` simplification may not match filesystem traversal through symlinks. hbrtl hb_dirsepadd hb_dirsepdel hb_pathjoin hb_pathrelativize cPath String Path string to normalize. String cNormalizedPath Normalized path string, or a null string when `<cPath>` is not a string.

records.name: hb_PathRelativize
records.summary: Attempts to express a target path relative to a base path.
records.search_text: hb_PathRelativize hb_PathRelativize Disks, Drives and Folders Disks, Drives and Folders File Functions hb_PathRelativize(<cPathBase>,<cPathTarget>,[<lForceRelative>]) => <cRelativePath> Attempts to express a target path relative to a base path. Attempts to express a target path relative to a base path. `<cPathBase>` and `<cPathTarget>` must both be strings; otherwise the function returns a null string. The function first joins both paths to `hb_DirBase()`, splits them into path components, compares common leading components using file-pattern matching, and then returns a relative path when possible. If the paths are on different drive specifications, or if relative conversion is not possible, it returns the target path. `<lForceRelative>` defaults to `.T.` and allows the function to produce `..`-based relative paths when the constructed relative location can be resolved from the base directory. hbrtl hb_dirbase hb_filematch hb_pathjoin hb_pathnormalize cPathBase String Base path used as the reference for the relative path. cPathTarget String Target path to express relative to `<cPathBase>` when possible. lForceRelative Logic When `.T.`, allows construction of a `..`-based relative path when it resolves from the base directory. String cRelativePath Relative path when conversion is possible; otherwise the resolved target path, or a null string for invalid arguments.

records.name: HB_TOKENCOUNT
records.summary: Counts tokens in `<cString>` using a delimiter, optional quote handling, and optional double-quote-only handling.
records.search_text: HB_TOKENCOUNT HB_TOKENCOUNT String Byte Functions String Byte Functions String Codepage Functions Token Functions hb_TokenCount(<cString>,[<cDelimiter>],[<lSkipStrings>],[<lDoubleQuoteOnly>]) => <nTokens> Counts tokens in `<cString>` using a delimiter, optional quote handling, and optional double-quote-only handling. Counts tokens in `<cString>` using a delimiter, optional quote handling, and optional double-quote-only handling. When `<cDelimiter>` is omitted or not supplied as a non-empty string, spaces are used as the delimiter. If the delimiter argument is logical `.T.`, end-of-line sequences are treated as delimiters. hbrtl hb_atokens cString String String to tokenize. cDelimiter Any Delimiter string. If logical `.T.`, end-of-line sequences are delimiters. If omitted or empty, a space delimiter is used. lSkipStrings Logic When `.T.`, quoted substrings are respected so delimiters inside quotes do not split tokens. lDoubleQuoteOnly Logic When quote handling is enabled, restricts quote recognition to double quotes only. Numeric nTokens Number of tokens found. Returns `0` when the first argument is not a string.

records.name: HB_TOKENGET
records.summary: Returns the `<nToken>` token from `<cString>` using the same delimiter and quote-handling rules as `hb_TokenCount()`.
records.search_text: HB_TOKENGET HB_TOKENGET String Byte Functions String Byte Functions Token Functions hb_TokenGet(<cString>,<nToken>,[<cDelimiter>],[<lSkipStrings>],[<lDoubleQuoteOnly>]) => <cToken> Returns the `<nToken>` token from `<cString>` using the same delimiter and quote-handling rules as `hb_TokenCount()`. Returns the `<nToken>` token from `<cString>` using the same delimiter and quote-handling rules as `hb_TokenCount()`. hbrtl hb_atokens cString String String to tokenize. nToken Numeric One-based token number to return. cDelimiter Any Delimiter string, logical `.T.` for end-of-line delimiters, or omitted for spaces. lSkipStrings Logic When `.T.`, quoted substrings are respected while scanning. lDoubleQuoteOnly Logic When quote handling is enabled, restricts quote recognition to double quotes only. String cToken Requested token text. Returns an empty string when the token is not found or the first argument is not a string.

records.name: HB_TOKENPTR
records.summary: Returns the next token from `<cString>` starting at zero-based position `<nPos>`, and updates `<nPos>` by reference to the next scan position.
records.search_text: HB_TOKENPTR HB_TOKENPTR String Byte Functions String Byte Functions Token Functions hb_TokenPtr(<cString>,[<@nPos>],[<cDelimiter>],[<lSkipStrings>],[<lDoubleQuoteOnly>]) => <cToken> Returns the next token from `<cString>` starting at zero-based position `<nPos>`, and updates `<nPos>` by reference to the next scan position. Returns the next token from `<cString>` starting at zero-based position `<nPos>`, and updates `<nPos>` by reference to the next scan position. hbrtl hb_atokens cString String String to tokenize. nPos Numeric Zero-based starting byte position. Receives the next position for a subsequent call, or a position past the end when no further token is available. cDelimiter Any Delimiter string, logical `.T.` for end-of-line delimiters, or omitted for spaces. lSkipStrings Logic When `.T.`, quoted substrings are respected while scanning. lDoubleQuoteOnly Logic When quote handling is enabled, restricts quote recognition to double quotes only. String cToken Next token text. Returns an empty string when no token is available or the first argument is not a string.

records.name: HB_VFOPEN

records.search_text: HB_VFOPEN HB_VFOPEN File Functions File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_vfOpen(<[@]cFileName>,[<nModeAttr>]) => <pHandle> Opens a virtual file and returns a virtual file handle. Opens a virtual file and returns a virtual file handle. fileio.ch hbrtl hb_vfclose hb_vfexists hb_vfread hb_vfwrite cFileName String File name to open. If passed by reference, it can receive the resolved file name used by the virtual file system. nModeAttr Numeric File open mode and attributes. Use `FO_*` constants from `fileio.ch`. Pointer pHandle Virtual file handle. If `NIL` is returned, the file could not be opened.

records.name: HB_VFREAD
records.summary: Reads bytes from a virtual file handle.
records.search_text: HB_VFREAD HB_VFREAD File Functions File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_vfRead(<pHandle>,<@cBuffer>,[<nToRead>],[<nTimeout>]) => <nBytesRead> Reads bytes from a virtual file handle. Reads bytes from a virtual file handle. hbrtl ferror hb_vfclose hb_vfopen hb_vfreadlen hb_vfwrite pHandle Pointer Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API. cBuffer String Writable string buffer that receives the bytes read. nToRead Numeric Maximum number of bytes to read. If omitted, Harbour uses the current length of `<cBuffer>`. nTimeout Numeric Read timeout passed to the virtual file backend. Numeric nBytesRead Number of bytes read. Returns `FS_ERROR` on read error.

records.name: HB_VFREADAT
records.summary: Reads bytes from a virtual file handle at a specific file offset into a string buffer passed by reference.
records.search_text: HB_VFREADAT HB_VFREADAT File Functions File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_vfReadAt(<pHandle>,<@cBuffer>,[<nToRead>],[<nAtOffset>]) => <nBytesRead> Reads bytes from a virtual file handle at a specific file offset into a string buffer passed by reference. Reads bytes from a virtual file handle at a specific file offset into a string buffer passed by reference. `<cBuffer>` must be a writable string passed by reference. `<nToRead>` optionally limits the number of bytes to read; if omitted, Harbour uses the current length of `<cBuffer>`. `<nAtOffset>` optionally specifies the file offset; the default is `-1`, which is passed to the virtual file backend. hbrtl hb_vfread hb_vfwriteat pHandle Pointer Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API. cBuffer String Writable string buffer that receives the bytes read. nToRead Numeric Maximum number of bytes to read. If omitted, Harbour uses the current length of `<cBuffer>`. nAtOffset Numeric File offset to read from. Numeric nBytesRead Number of bytes read. Returns `FS_ERROR` on read error.

records.name: HB_VFREADLEN
records.summary: Reads a requested byte count from a virtual file and returns the bytes read.
records.search_text: HB_VFREADLEN HB_VFREADLEN File Functions File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_vfReadLen(<pHandle>,<nToRead>,[<nTimeout>]) => <cBuffer> Reads a requested byte count from a virtual file and returns the bytes read. Reads a requested byte count from a virtual file and returns the bytes read. hbrtl hb_vfopen hb_vfread hb_vfwrite pHandle Pointer Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API. nToRead Numeric Maximum number of bytes to read. nTimeout Numeric Read timeout passed to the virtual file backend. String cBuffer Bytes read from the file. Returns a null string when `<nToRead>` is less than or equal to `0`, or when a read error occurs.

records.name: HB_VFTIMEGET
records.summary: Retrieves the timestamp of a file through Harbour's virtual file system.
records.search_text: HB_VFTIMEGET HB_VFTIMEGET File Functions File Functions hb_vfTimeGet(<cFileName>,<@tsDateTime>,[<@cTime>]) => <lOk> Retrieves the timestamp of a file through Harbour's virtual file system. Retrieves the timestamp of a file through Harbour's virtual file system. hbrtl hb_vftimeset cFileName String File name whose timestamp is retrieved. tsDateTime Datetime Receives the file timestamp when `<cTime>` is omitted. If `<cTime>` is supplied by reference, this parameter receives only the date part. cTime String Optional time output string. When supplied by reference, Harbour stores the date in parameter 2 and the time string in this parameter. Logic lOk Returns `.T.` if the timestamp is retrieved successfully; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_VFWRITE
records.summary: Writes bytes to a virtual file handle.
records.search_text: HB_VFWRITE HB_VFWRITE File Functions File Functions Low Level File Functions hb_vfWrite(<pHandle>,<cBuffer>,[<nToWrite>],[<nTimeout>]) => <nBytesWritten> Writes bytes to a virtual file handle. Writes bytes to a virtual file handle. hbrtl hb_vfopen hb_vfread hb_vfreadlen pHandle Pointer Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API. cBuffer String String data to write. nToWrite Numeric Maximum number of bytes to write. If omitted, Harbour uses `Len(cBuffer)`. nTimeout Numeric Write timeout passed to the virtual file backend. Numeric nBytesWritten Number of bytes written. Returns `FS_ERROR` on write error. Returns `0` if `<cBuffer>` is not a string.

records.name: HB_VFWRITEAT

Writes bytes from a string to a virtual file handle at a specific file offset. `<cBuffer>` is the data to write. `<nToWrite>` optionally limits the number of bytes written; if omitted, Harbour uses the full length of `<cBuffer>`. `<nAtOffset>` optionally specifies the file offset; the default is `-1`, which is passed to the virtual file backend. hbrtl hb_vfreadat hb_vfwrite pHandle Pointer Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API. cBuffer String String data to write. nToWrite Numeric Maximum number of bytes to write. If omitted, Harbour uses `Len(cBuffer)`. nAtOffset Numeric File offset to write at. Numeric nBytesWritten Number of bytes written. Returns `FS_ERROR` on write error. Returns `0` if `<cBuffer>` is not a string.

NIL Does not return a useful value.

records.name: HB_WEEK
records.summary: Returns the ISO 8601 week number for a date or timestamp value.
records.search_text: HB_WEEK HB_WEEK Date and Time Functions Date and Time Functions hb_Week(<dDate>,[<@nYear>],[<@nDayOfWeek>]) => <nWeek> Returns the ISO 8601 week number for a date or timestamp value. Returns the ISO 8601 week number for a date or timestamp value. If `<nYear>` is passed by reference, it receives the ISO week-year. If `<nDayOfWeek>` is passed by reference, it receives the ISO day-of-week number. If the first argument is not a date or timestamp value, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error. hbrtl dow week woy dDate Datetime Date or timestamp value. nYear Numeric Receives the ISO week-year. nDayOfWeek Numeric Receives the ISO day-of-week number. Numeric nWeek ISO 8601 week number.

records.name: HB_WILDMATCH
records.summary: Matches a string against a wildcard pattern.
records.search_text: HB_WILDMATCH HB_WILDMATCH String Byte Functions String Byte Functions String Codepage Functions hb_WildMatch(<cPattern>,<cValue>,[<lExact>]) => <lMatch> Matches a string against a wildcard pattern. Matches a string against a wildcard pattern. hbrtl hb_filematch hb_wildmatchi cPattern String Wildcard pattern containing `?` and `*`. cValue String Text value to compare with the pattern. lExact Logic When `.T.`, the whole value must match the pattern. When `.F.` or omitted, prefix-style wildcard matching is used. Logic lMatch Returns `.T.` if `<cValue>` matches `<cPattern>` according to the selected wildcard mode; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_WILDMATCHI
records.summary: Case-insensitive wildcard string match.
records.search_text: HB_WILDMATCHI HB_WILDMATCHI String Byte Functions String Byte Functions String Codepage Functions hb_WildMatchI(<cPattern>,<cValue>) => <lMatch> Case-insensitive wildcard string match. Case-insensitive wildcard string match. hbrtl hb_filematch hb_wildmatch cPattern String Wildcard pattern containing `?` and `*`. cValue String Text value to compare with the pattern. Logic lMatch Returns `.T.` if `<cValue>` matches `<cPattern>` using case-insensitive exact wildcard matching; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_WRITEINI
records.summary: Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle.
records.search_text: HB_WRITEINI HB_WRITEINI INI Files INI Files hb_iniWrite=>hb_iniWrite(<xFileName>,<hIni>,[<cCommentBegin>],[<cCommentEnd>],[<lAutoMain>]) => <lSuccess> Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle. Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle. `hb_iniWrite()` first calls `hb_iniWriteStr()` to build the INI text. `<xFileName>` may be a filename string, in which case the function creates/overwrites the file and closes it, or a numeric file handle, in which case the handle is used but not closed. The function returns `.F.` if INI text cannot be generated, the file argument is invalid, the file cannot be created/opened, or the full text cannot be written. hbrtl Logic lSuccess `.T.` if the generated INI text is written successfully; otherwise `.F.`.

records.search_text: HEXTONUM HEXTONUM Conversion Functions Conversion Functions hb_HexToNum=>hb_HexToNum(<cHex>) => <nNumber> Converts a hexadecimal string to a numeric value. Converts a hexadecimal string to a numeric value. Leading spaces are skipped. Hex digits may be uppercase or lowercase. If a non-hexadecimal character is encountered after the leading spaces, the result is reset to `0`. If the argument is not a string, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error. hbrtl hextostr strtohex Numeric nNumber Numeric value represented by `<cHex>`, or `0` when an invalid hexadecimal character is encountered.

records.name: HEXTOSTR
records.summary: Converts hexadecimal text to a byte string.
records.search_text: HEXTOSTR HEXTOSTR Conversion Functions Conversion Functions hb_HexToStr=>hb_HexToStr(<cHex>) => <cString> Converts hexadecimal text to a byte string. Converts hexadecimal text to a byte string. Non-hexadecimal characters are ignored. Hex digits are consumed in pairs; if fewer than two hex digits are available, the function returns a null string. If the argument is not a string, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error. hbrtl hextonum strtohex String cString Decoded byte string, or a null string when there are not enough hexadecimal digits to form at least one byte.

records.name: MEMOLINE
records.summary: Extracts one formatted line from a character string or memo text.
records.search_text: MEMOLINE MEMOLINE Memo Functions Memo Functions String Codepage Functions MemoLine(<cString>,[<nLineLength>],[<nLineNumber>],[<nTabSize>],[<lWrap>],[<xEOLs>],[<lPad>]) => <cLine> Extracts one formatted line from a character string or memo text. Extracts one formatted line from a character string or memo text. Formatting uses a target line width, tab expansion, optional word wrapping, and optional custom end-of-line markers. By default, `<nLineLength>` is `79`, `<nLineNumber>` is `1`, `<nTabSize>` is `4`, `<lWrap>` is `.T.`, and `<lPad>` is `.T.`. `<xEOLs>` is a Harbour extension: it may be a string end-of-line marker or an array of string markers. If no custom marker is supplied, Harbour uses the current SET EOL value or the console newline. `<lPad>` is an undocumented compatibility parameter and should be treated as legacy behavior. hbrtl mlcount mlpos cString String Source character string or memo text. nLineLength Numeric Formatted line width used when splitting the text into lines. nLineNumber Numeric One-based formatted line number to extract. nTabSize Numeric Tab expansion width. Values outside the useful range are normalized by the implementation. lWrap Logic When `.T.`, wraps at word boundaries where possible; when `.F.`, uses fixed-width line breaking. xEOLs Any Optional Harbour extension specifying custom end-of-line marker text or an array of marker strings. lPad Logic When `.T.`, pads the returned line to the requested line width. This is an undocumented compatibility parameter. String cLine Formatted line text. With default padding, the returned string is padded to `<nLineLength>`.

records.name: ORDCONDSET
records.summary: Sets the pending order-condition information used by the next order creation operation in the current work area.
records.search_text: ORDCONDSET ORDCONDSET Index Function Index Function ordCondSet([<cForCondition>],[<bForCondition>],[<lAllRecords>],[<bWhileCondition>],[<bEval>],[<nInterval>],[<xStartRecord>],[<nNext>],[<xRecord>],[<lRest>],[<lDescend>],[<lCompound>],[<lAdditive>],[<lCurrent>],[<lCustom>],[<lNoOptimize>],[<cWhileCondition>],[<lTemporary>],[<lUseFilter>],[<lExclusive>]) => <lSuccess> Sets the pending order-condition information used by the next order creation operation in the current work area. Sets the pending order-condition information used by the next order creation operation in the current work area. The condition can include FOR/WHILE expressions, scope controls, evaluation/progress behavior, descending/custom/temporary flags, and RDD-specific options. Returns `.T.` when the current RDD accepts the condition information; returns `.F.` when there is no current work area or the RDD rejects the condition. hbrdd cForCondition String Optional textual FOR condition used to limit records included when the order is built. bForCondition CodeBlock Optional FOR condition code block used to limit records included when the order is built. lAllRecords Logic Controls whether all records are eligible before other conditions or scope options are applied. Defaults to `.T.`. bWhileCondition CodeBlock Optional WHILE condition code block. When supplied, the source also treats the order condition as REST-scoped. bEval CodeBlock Optional evaluation/progress code block passed to the RDD while building the order. nInterval Numeric Evaluation interval or step count associated with `<bEval>`. xStartRecord Any Optional starting record identity or RDD-specific scope item. The source accepts any non-`NIL` item. nNext Numeric Optional maximum number of records to process. xRecord Any Optional record identity or RDD-specific scope item. The source accepts any non-`NIL` item. lRest Logic When `.T.`, limits processing to the REST scope from the current record. lDescend Logic When `.T.`, requests a descending order. lCompound Logic Legacy compatibility compound flag. The source notes that this argument is always `NIL` in Clipper 5.3 and is the compound flag in Clipper 5.2. lAdditive Logic When `.T.`, requests additive order behavior so existing orders remain open where the RDD supports it. lCurrent Logic When `.T.`, requests use of the current record or current scope while building the order. lCustom Logic When `.T.`, requests custom-order behavior for RDDs that support manually maintained orders. lNoOptimize Logic When `.T.`, disables RDD optimization of the condition. cWhileCondition String Optional textual WHILE condition used to limit records included when the order is built. lTemporary Logic When `.T.`, requests temporary or memory-backed order handling. The source leaves the actual storage decision to the RDD. lUseFilter Logic When `.T.`, requests that the RDD respect active `SET FILTER` and `SET DELETED` state while building the order. lExclusive Logic Harbour extension flag requesting an order/index that is not shared with other clients. Logic lSuccess `.T.` when the pending order condition is accepted by the current RDD; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: ORDCREATE

records.search_text: ORDCREATE ORDCREATE Index Function Index Function ordCreate(<cOrderBagName>,[<cOrderName>],<cKeyExpr>,[<bKeyExpr>],[<lUnique>],[<cConstrName>],[<cTargetName>],[<aRelationKey>],[<lEnabled>]) => <NIL> Creates an order/tag for the current work area in the specified order bag. Creates an order/tag for the current work area in the specified order bag. `<cOrderBagName>` identifies the order bag or index file, `<cOrderName>` optionally names the order, and `<cKeyExpr>`/`<bKeyExpr>` define the key expression. If `<lUnique>` is omitted, Harbour uses the current `SET UNIQUE` setting. Optional constraint parameters are used only when the constraint name, target name, and relation-key array are all supplied. hbrdd cOrderBagName String Order bag or index file name in which the order is created. cOrderName String Optional order/tag name within the order bag. cKeyExpr String String form of the order key expression. bKeyExpr CodeBlock Optional executable code block for the key expression. lUnique Logic When supplied, controls whether the order is unique. When omitted, the current `SET UNIQUE` value is used. cConstrName String Optional relational-integrity constraint name. Used only when the target name and relation-key array are also supplied. cTargetName String Optional target relation/table name for relational-integrity metadata. aRelationKey Array Optional relation-key array used with the constraint and target names. lEnabled Logic Enables the constraint when complete constraint metadata is supplied. NIL Always returns `NIL` when execution completes normally. Invalid parameters or a missing current work area raise a runtime error.
